title: Job Evaluation
content: **[JOB EVALUATION & SALARY BENCHMARKING]{.underline}**
**A. Service Overview**

Paying the wrong salaries costs you twice---you either lose good people to competitors or waste money overpaying the wrong roles . Job evaluation and salary benchmarking in South Africa give you a fair, defensible pay structure that keeps staff, controls costs, and protects you from equal pay disputes at the CCMA .
**B. Detailed Description**

Job evaluation grades every role in your organization based on what the job requires---not who's doing it or how well they negotiate . Salary benchmarking compares your pay to the South African market so you know if you're competitive . Together, they create a pay structure you can defend, afford, and trust .
What we do:
Job Analysis -- We study each job to understand what it really involves: tasks, decisions, responsibilities, working conditions, and the skills needed .

Job Descriptions -- We write clear, detailed job descriptions that define duties, authority, and reporting lines. Every job description is signed off by the employee and manager to prevent disputes later .

Job Grading -- We use the Paterson Job Grading System (South Africa's most widely used method) to grade jobs into a fair hierarchy based on decision-making complexity . Jobs are graded from the bottom up, ensuring internal fairness across your entire organization .

Salary Benchmarking -- We compare your salaries to current market data for similar roles in your industry and region across South Africa . You'll know exactly where you're overpaying, underpaying, or spot-on .
What you get:
🔹 Clear job descriptions for every role
🔹 A fair job grading structure with documented rationales for every grade
🔹 Market salary ranges for each grade
🔹 A roadmap showing which salaries to adjust and when
🔹 Protection from equal pay claims and CCMA disputes
🔹 Better retention---staff stay when they're paid fairly
C. Who Is This For?
✅ Businesses with no formal pay structure who set salaries based on gut feel or negotiation
✅ Companies with staff complaining about unfair pay or favouritism
✅ Growing businesses in Gauteng and across South Africa adding new roles and unsure what to pay
✅ Employers preparing for Employment Equity audits needing to prove equal pay for equal work
✅ Organizations with high staff turnover where people leave for better pay
✅ Private schools needing fair salary structures for educators and support staff
✅ Companies facing equal pay claims or CCMA remuneration disputes
✅ Businesses preparing for mergers, acquisitions, or investor due diligence
**D. Why Choose OptiHR for This Service?**
🚨 Legal expertise built in -- Our owner is an admitted attorney who understands South Africa's equal pay laws. Your grading system will be CCMA-proof .
🚨 We use the Paterson System -- South Africa's most trusted job evaluation method, recognized by unions, government, and the CCMA .
🚨 We do the work, not just advise -- We write job descriptions, chair grading committees, benchmark salaries, and help you implement changes .
🚨 We prevent job inflation -- Employees sometimes exaggerate their roles to get higher grades. We identify inflated job descriptions and keep grading objective .
🚨 Market data you can trust -- We use current South African salary data from reputable sources and can arrange specialist salary surveys for your industry .
**E. Case Study or Testimonial**
The School Losing Teachers Every Year

An independent school in Gauteng with 80 staff couldn't keep experienced teachers. Every year, good educators left for better pay at competing schools. The problem? No formal salary structure. Pay was based on negotiation, not fairness. Some teachers doing the same work earned vastly different salaries .
The leadership was also worried about Employment Equity compliance. They had no way to prove their pay decisions weren't discriminatory .
OptiHR stepped in:
✔ We analyzed every job and wrote clear job descriptions
✔ We graded all roles using the Paterson System
✔ We benchmarked salaries against similar independent schools across South Africa
✔ We found 15 staff paid below market and 8 paid too much
✔ We built transparent salary ranges for each grade
✔ We created a two-year plan to bring underpaid staff up to market rates while freezing overpaid salaries
The results:
🔹 Teacher retention improved 40% in the first year
🔹 The school passed an Employment Equity audit with full pay equity compliance
🔹 Recruitment became easier---competitive packages attracted better candidates
🔹 Staff trust increased---everyone understood how pay decisions were made

"We thought fair pay meant paying everyone the same. OptiHR showed us that fair means paying based on what the job requires---not negotiation skills or personal relationships. Our teachers stopped leaving." -- School Principal, Gauteng
**F. Compliance & Legal Considerations**
Job evaluation and salary benchmarking protect you from South Africa's labour laws :
📌 Employment Equity Act (EEA) -- Requires equal pay for work of equal value. A job grading system proves you're not discriminating .
📌 Code of Good Practice on Equal Pay -- Guides how to assess work value and justify pay differences. Job evaluation provides this proof .
📌 CCMA disputes -- Many unfair labour practice cases involve remuneration. A defensible grading system reduces your risk .
📌 EEA4 and EEA9 Reporting -- Job grading aligns with occupational levels required in Employment Equity reports .
📌 National Minimum Wage Act -- Ensures no one earns below R28.79 per hour (March 2025 rate) .
📌 Labour Relations Act (LRA) -- Transparent pay structures reduce remuneration disputes .
📌 Fair Pay Bill (2025) -- Proposed legislation requiring salary ranges in job ads. Benchmarking prepares you now .
**G. Pricing or Engagement Models**
Full Job Evaluation & Salary Benchmarking
Complete job analysis, job descriptions, Paterson grading, market benchmarking across South Africa, salary structure design, and implementation support .
Best for: Organizations with no formal pay structure or facing pay equity issues.
Job Evaluation Only
Job descriptions and Paterson grading without market benchmarking .
Best for: Organizations wanting internal fairness first.
Salary Benchmarking Only
Market analysis of your current salaries with South African industry data and adjustment recommendations .
Best for: Businesses with grading in place but unsure if they're paying competitively.
Grading Committee Support
We chair your grading committee, train your team, or act as your full grading committee .
Best for: Companies managing job evaluation internally but needing expert guidance.
Targeted Role Benchmarking
Benchmarking specific roles (executives, specialists, hard-to-fill positions) .
Best for: Addressing retention issues in key roles.
Pricing depends on organization size and complexity. Contact us for a South Africa-tailored quote.
H. FAQ (1--3 for each service)
Q1: How long does job evaluation and salary benchmarking take?
For small businesses (under 50 employees), 4--6 weeks. Larger organizations may take 8--10 weeks . We work efficiently to minimize disruption to your South African operations.
Q2: Will employees' salaries change immediately?

Not necessarily. We provide recommendations, but you control timing. Most organizations phase changes over 1--2 years to manage budget impact . Underpaid staff are prioritized; overpaid staff have salaries frozen until ranges catch up .
Q3: What if employees disagree with their job grades?

We prevent disputes by ensuring employees and managers agree on job descriptions before grading . All job descriptions are signed off. Every grading decision is documented with clear rationales, making it defensible at the CCMA .
**I. Call-to-Action**
Pay fairly. Pay competitively. Know where you stand in the South African market.
Job evaluation and salary benchmarking give you fairness, control, and protection from costly equal pay claims .
👉 Request a Free Job Evaluation Consultation and find out if your pay structure is helping or hurting your business in South Africa.
